evaluator: fresh independent full-epic Level 2 evaluator (after all slices merge)
approval_notes: Orchestrator-directed F005 Slice 3. Build ONE slice, open ONE PR, do NOT merge, do NOT flip any passes flag. F005 stays passes:false; a separate fresh-context full-epic Level 2 close-out owns the flip after all slices merge.

Builds on Slice 1 (merged 405264a: Cloudflare official extraction + config->DB sync + runner + migration 0007) and Slice 2 (merged d377d0a: deterministic gated publication path; Offer/OfferVersion/Quota + Z0 classify + confidence/classification/gate in OfferVersion.material_facts JSONB).

Standing autonomy boundaries: may branch/implement/test/commit/push/open ONE PR; may NOT merge, flip any passes flag, add a Python/npm dependency, add a migration, weaken any existing test/fixture/CHECK/trigger, touch trg_offer_version_immutable or the two 0006 separation triggers, or add an LLM/network/user-controlled-URL path.
objective: Expose the PUBLISHED catalogue over HTTP as a READ-ONLY (GET-only) provider/catalogue API so the S4 web experience and API consumers can read it, reflecting the real data the S1 scan + S2 publish produced. Deliver end-to-end and genuinely working (no stubs/fake data/TODO-backed core behavior). Scope items: (1) providers list + provider detail (Cloudflare) with metadata; (2) category/service states for a provider (offers grouped by service/category, each with its current Z0 state); (3) per published offer: its current OfferVersion, the Z0 zero_cost_class + human-readable reasons (from material_facts.classification) + quota rows; (4) the official evidence backing each offer (source + snapshot/provenance + offer_version link) and a plain-language CONFIDENCE LABEL derived from material_facts.confidence (numeric only in an advanced/detail block, never the primary field); (5) OfferVersion history (append-only) + ChangeEvents (added/modified) so the UI can show what changed and when; (6) completeness + freshness signals per offer/provider (reuse what S2 computed in material_facts.confidence_signals / Provider score columns). Reads persisted rows only; deterministic; no LLM; 'unknown is better than guessed'.
scope: apps/api/app/db.py: add a read-only, request-scoped SQLAlchemy Session dependency (get_sessionmaker cached on the lazy engine + a get_session() generator) so FastAPI routes can query without any write path. No engine/settings behaviour change.
apps/api/app/read_api/__init__.py (new package): export the router + public schema/service symbols.

apps/api/app/read_api/confidence.py (new): pure numeric->plain-language confidence LABEL mapping (high/medium/low/unknown) using the version's stored gate thresholds (material_facts.gate.automatic_threshold/uncertain_threshold) when present, else documented fixed fallbacks. Never exposes the numeric as the primary field.

apps/api/app/read_api/schemas.py (new): Pydantic response models (ProviderSummary, ProviderDetail, CategoryState/ServiceState, OfferSummary, OfferDetail with confidence LABEL + nested advanced{score,signals}, QuotaOut, EvidenceOut, OfferVersionOut, ChangeEventOut, CompletenessFreshness). Numeric confidence appears ONLY inside the advanced/detail block per D039.

apps/api/app/read_api/service.py (new): pure serialization from ORM rows + OfferVersion.material_facts JSONB (classification.reasons, confidence, confidence_signals, gate) into the schemas; honest nulls/'unknown' when a value is absent (never fabricated).

apps/api/app/read_api/queries.py (new): read-only SQLAlchemy SELECTs (providers, provider by slug, services+offers+latest version for a provider, offer by id + latest version + quotas, official offer_version-linked evidence + source + snapshot, offer_version history + change events, per-provider completeness/freshness aggregation from latest published versions). NEVER selects candidate/discovery_candidate; NEVER writes.

apps/api/app/read_api/router.py (new): a FastAPI APIRouter with GET-only endpoints under /catalogue: GET /catalogue/providers; GET /catalogue/providers/{provider_slug}; GET /catalogue/providers/{provider_slug}/category-states; GET /catalogue/providers/{provider_slug}/offers; GET /catalogue/offers/{offer_id}; GET /catalogue/offers/{offer_id}/evidence; GET /catalogue/offers/{offer_id}/history. Inputs are internal identifiers only (provider slug, offer id) - no URL/host/endpoint is ever accepted from the caller (no SSRF surface). 404 with a credential-free message for unknown ids.
apps/api/app/main.py: app.include_router(read_api.router). No other behaviour change; /health* preserved.

tests/unit/test_read_api.py (new): FastAPI TestClient with the get_session dependency overridden by an in-memory/fake seeded session (offline, no live DB); asserts each endpoint's shape, the confidence LABEL mapping (incl. unknown fallback), read-only (only GET verbs; POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE -> 405), that community/candidate data is never present in any response, and that numeric confidence appears only in the advanced block.

tests/integration/test_read_api.py (new, live PG, skipped without DATABASE_URL): run the S1 scan + S2 publish to create a real published Cloudflare offer, then exercise the read query path proving providers list/detail, category states, offer + Z0 class + reasons + quotas, official evidence + confidence label, version history + change events, and completeness/freshness all reflect the published data.
docs/ARCHITECTURE.md: document the read-only catalogue API (endpoints, read-only + no-SSRF posture, confidence label from material_facts). Append an agent-state/progress.md handoff.
out_of_scope: Any write/mutation/publish endpoint. Only GET endpoints; no POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE.
Any endpoint that accepts a URL/host/endpoint from the caller and fetches it (no SSRF surface); inputs are internal identifiers only.
Any new Python or npm dependency; no pyproject/requirements*/package.json change (requirements-sync stays green; reuse FastAPI/SQLAlchemy/pydantic).
Any database migration (read API over existing tables). If a hard blocker requiring a migration is discovered, STOP and report rather than inventing one.
Flipping F005 passes:true or editing any feature_list.json passes flag (reserved for the post-merge full-epic Level 2 close-out).
Weakening OfferVersion immutability (trg_offer_version_immutable) or the two 0006 separation triggers; touching publish/classify semantics.
Exposing community/discovery_candidate or pre-publication candidate data as published catalogue data.
The S4 web UI itself (React pages/components), the adviser, RSS, admin, and hosting-proof APIs (later slices).
acceptance_criteria: READ-ONLY: every new endpoint is a GET that only reads; no endpoint writes/mutates/publishes; a non-GET verb on a catalogue path returns 405.
No SSRF surface: no endpoint accepts a URL/host/endpoint from the caller; inputs are internal identifiers (provider slug, offer id) only.
Providers: list providers and get one provider (Cloudflare) with its metadata; unknown slug -> 404 with a credential-free message.
Category/service states: a provider's offers grouped by service/category, each with its current Z0 zero_cost_class.
Offers + Z0 reasons: for each published offer, its current OfferVersion, the Z0 zero_cost_class + human-readable reasons (from material_facts.classification.reasons), and quota rows.

Evidence + confidence: the official evidence backing each offer (source + snapshot provenance + offer_version link) and a plain-language confidence LABEL derived from material_facts.confidence; the numeric score appears only in an advanced/detail block, never as the primary field.
History: the OfferVersion history for an offer (append-only versions) + the ChangeEvents (added/modified) so the UI can show what changed and when.
Completeness/freshness: completeness + freshness signals per offer (from material_facts.confidence_signals) and per provider (aggregated / Provider score columns) are exposed.
'unknown is better than guessed': a missing value is represented honestly (null / 'unknown' label); no field is fabricated.
No community leakage: no response ever contains candidate/discovery_candidate/community data.
No new dependency, no migration; requirements-sync stays green; immutability + separation triggers intact; publish/classify semantics untouched.
Regression green: scripts/test.ps1 and scripts/check.ps1 -NodeAudit pass offline (ruff lint+format, pytest, prettier, eslint, detect-secrets, pip-audit, npm audit, requirements-sync).
F005 remains passes:false; a fresh-context independent full-epic Level 2 evaluator (post all-slices merge) owns any status change.

integration_commands: Fresh live DB cycle on alternate host ports (POSTGRES_PORT=55432, API_PORT=8010, WEB_PORT=8090, DATABASE_URL=postgresql+psycopg://atlas:atlas@localhost:55432/atlas): docker compose down --volumes -> scripts/stack-up.ps1 --build (fresh DB applies 0001-0007) -> run S1 scan + S2 publish (python -m app.ingest.runner --publish against the captured Cloudflare fixtures) -> scripts/stack-smoke.ps1 -> pytest tests/integration/test_read_api.py with DATABASE_URL set -> curl the new endpoints over HTTP (API_PORT=8010) confirming providers list/detail, category states, offer+Z0+reasons+quota, evidence+confidence LABEL, version history+change events, completeness/freshness all reflect the published data; confirm NO write endpoint exists and no endpoint accepts a fetchable URL -> docker compose down --volumes.

risks.data_migration: None: Slice 3 adds NO migration. It reads existing tables (0001..0007). Migration head stays 0007; compare_metadata drift stays [].
risks.security_privacy: Read-only GET surface; no writes; no LLM; no network egress; no user-controlled URLs (inputs are internal identifiers only, so no SSRF surface). No secrets in source/tests/logs. Only published offer/offer_version/official-evidence rows are exposed; community/candidate/discovery_candidate data is never queried.
risks.portability: Reuses FastAPI/SQLAlchemy/pydantic already present; no new dependency; amd64/arm64 unaffected.
risks.zero_cost_safety: The API reports the Z0 class and reasons exactly as the S2 engine persisted them; it never re-derives or upgrades a class and never fabricates a confidence/label. Unknown/absent values are surfaced as null/'unknown'.
risks.immutability: Read-only: no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE anywhere. trg_offer_version_immutable and the two 0006 separation triggers are untouched.
risks.regression: Additive router + a read-only session dependency; /health* and existing behaviour unchanged. The publish=False runner default and all existing tests are preserved.
risks.supply_chain: No new Python or npm dependency; requirements-sync stays green; stdlib + already-present deps only.

notes: Orchestrator directed F005 Slice 3 (read-only catalogue/provider API), then STOP for a fresh-context full-epic Level 2 evaluation. Do NOT flip passes; do NOT merge; do NOT add a migration or a dependency; read-only GET only; no user-controlled URLs; do NOT weaken immutability/separation; do NOT expose community data.
